description:
[SUPERSEDED 2026-05-26 — degraded-slice framing identified by user; FEAT-006 (north-star) carries the actual scope. Superseded by FEAT-006 decomposition: TASK-088 (work-order block kind), TASK-089 (pi-agent-dispatch extension + FEAT-005 capability composition), TASK-090 (real-check tail + attested commit), TASK-091 (assembly + end-to-end loop wiring + runtime demo). The 'lightweight scripts/orchestrator/ POC' framing of this task was LLM-laziness substituting a Claude-Code-side script demo for the user-stated intent of an in-pi-orchestrator end-to-end loop. The in-pi orchestrator (the harness-confined agent in pi launched via our script) cannot use scripts/orchestrator/ because those are Claude-Code-side; the actual intent requires the pi-agent-dispatch extension building pi.registerTool('call-agent',…) so the in-pi orchestrator sees the dispatch surface in its tool registry. status=cancelled (tasks schema has no 'superseded' enum value); the four successor tasks carry the actual scope of what this would have proven and more. Original description preserved below as historical record.]

--- ORIGINAL DESCRIPTION (historical, pre-supersession) ---

POC — prove the context→jit concept end-to-end. The in-pi orchestrator (the main harness-constrained agent in pi, launched via our script) authors a scoped agent spec via pi-context tooling → persists → dispatches via pi-jit-agents library executeAgent → captures typed result → real-check verdict. Demonstrates the architectural shape works on a freshly-authored spec against a real LLM via pi-ai, with the operation-granular tool grant + clamp from TASK-081 in effect. Lightweight scope: NOT the full pi-agent-dispatch extension build (that is downstream FEAT-004/005 work after POC validates the shape). Scope is the smallest end-to-end demonstration: an author surface (scripts/orchestrator/author-agent.ts that writes a .agent.yaml + validates against jit-agents AgentSpec schema), a dispatch invocation (direct call to @davidorex/pi-jit-agents executeAgent — orchestrator-direct-use per JI-021), and a verification path (real-check on emitted artifacts: git diff / event stream / runtime demo). Authored agent uses a bounded tool grant (e.g. read-only — minimal grant proving clamp works); subagent invoked via pi -p with that grant; result captured; clamp empirically verified via attempt to exceed grant + observation of refusal. Depends on TASK-081 landing first (canonical agent layer + executeAgent grant/clamp must exist to dispatch into). Does NOT depend on TASK-082/083 (POC bypasses pi-workflows + pi-behavior-monitors entirely; orchestrator dispatches direct against jit-agents library). Filed 2026-05-26 per user direction (sequence: pi-jit-agents element first → POC → consumer cascade in 082/083).
acceptance_criteria: scripts/orchestrator/author-agent.ts exists; writes a .agent.yaml spec to a target path; validates the written spec against the jit-agents AgentSpec schema before declaring success; CLI signature matches existing scripts/orchestrator pattern (--name, --tools, --target, --writer)
Orchestrator authors a minimal scoped agent (e.g. read-only tool grant — proves the grant + clamp machinery from TASK-081 works)

Orchestrator dispatches the authored agent via direct @davidorex/pi-jit-agents executeAgent call (NOT via pi-workflows; NOT via pi-behavior-monitors; NOT via the not-yet-built pi-agent-dispatch extension — direct library import per JI-021)
Real LLM dispatch through pi-ai succeeds; typed result returned conforms to the agent's declared output schema

Clamp empirically verified: attempt to author an agent with a tool grant exceeding the orchestrator's own grant → refusal with explicit error per DEC-0047 clause (writer.kind=human required for widening; orchestrator is agent at this layer)

Real-check verdict: runtime-demo POC report at analysis/2026-05-26-poc-context-to-jit-runtime-demo.md captures (a) the authored spec content, (b) the dispatch invocation command + actual output, (c) the clamp-test outcome, (d) git diff showing the orchestrator-side surface — fresh-context adversarial probe re-verifies all four by reading the report + running the recorded commands
VALIDATION = real checks: build/check/test exit codes for the script + the runtime demo + adversarial reproduction; never LLM self-report (FGAP-102 / DEC-0047 clause 5)
notes: Filed 2026-05-26 per user direction. Sequence: TASK-081 first → THIS POC second → TASK-082/083 cascade third. POC bypasses pi-workflows + pi-behavior-monitors entirely (orchestrator dispatches direct against jit-agents library per JI-021). Validates the context→jit architectural shape before full FEAT-004/005 extension build. Successful POC unblocks the pi-agent-dispatch extension build with confidence.
